{{Quote|Collectively, the Patrol's about as bright as banthas armed with blasters, and just as subtle. I get the shivers just thinking about them.|[[Roark Slader]]|<ref name="PA">{{JournalCite|3|The Pentastar Alignment}}</ref>}}
The '''Pentastar Patrol''' was the military force that operated across the [[Pentastar Alignment]], a successor state to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] formed after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Organization==
The Pentastar Patrol was the military force of the [[Pentastar Alignment]].<ref name="TEA" /> It almost entirely consisted of an aggregation of former [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] units and bases that had been located in the territory that became the Alignment. However, in an effort to bolster its military force and reduce internal tension, nonhumans were allowed to serve in the Patrol. This move was not welcomed by the majority of the Patrol's officers, who were former Imperial military men steeped in the principles of [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]].<ref name="PA" />

As the Alignment maintained an isolationist policy, the Patrol mainly was concerned with stopping [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] raids. However, with the return of [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor Palpatine]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10]]-[[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the Patrol participated in his [[Pre–Shadow Hand campaign|renewed campaigns]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Following the death of Palpatine, and death of the Alignment's leader [[Ardus Kaine]], in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] the Alignment (along with its military forces) was absorbed into the [[Imperial Remnant]].<ref name="TEA" />

The Patrol's engineering divisions worked with [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] to design the [[Enforcer-class picket cruiser|''Enforcer''-class picket cruiser]]. The Patrol's officers were trained at the [[Pentastar Patrol Academy]].
